name: React best practices (performance hub)
description: Vercel Engineering (Jan 2026). Token-safe hub that routes to focused performance rule modules for React + Next.js: waterfalls, bundle size, server/client performance, re-render & rendering optimization, JS perf, advanced patterns.

metadata.intent: Apply high-impact React + Next.js performance best practices without loading a giant doc

must_not: Bulk-load every routine into context (keep it token-safe)
conventions: Open only the leaf module(s) relevant to the current performance symptom.

steps.description: If you see sequential awaits in server code (RSC, route handlers, server actions), open `react-best-practices-waterfalls`.
If build/dev is slow or bundles are heavy (Monaco, icons, MUI), open `react-best-practices-bundle-size`.
If RSC payloads feel big/slow, or server concurrency/caching is wrong, open `react-best-practices-server-performance`.
If client is refetching or duplicating listeners, open `react-best-practices-client-data-fetching`.
If the UI is janky due to re-renders/effects, open `react-best-practices-rerender-optimization`.
If hot-path JS is slow (loops, lookups, storage reads), open `react-best-practices-javascript-performance`.
If you’re using `useEffectEvent`, one-time init, stable subscriptions, open `react-best-practices-advanced-patterns`.
